page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, mapping to 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AES cryptography.

This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. In standardized performance testing, the ecs.re6p.xlarge demonstrates lower-tier compute capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU benchmark recorded a score of 1317.6, and the multi-core benchmark reached 3505.41, both placing the instance in the bottom 25% of tested servers.

The qualitative cost profile of the ecs.re6p.xlarge reflects its dedicated CPU allocation, which guarantees consistent compute resources without burstable degradation. However, the tradeoff lies in its lower benchmark-relative processing throughput compared to other dedicated hardware classes. Given the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the lack of local storage, this instance is structured for general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, lightweight application hosting, and development environments that require stable CPU performance without intensive parallel processing demands.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio with 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the instance exhibits modest compute capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores of 1317.6 and 3505.41, placing it in the bottom 25% of performance tiers. The ecs.re6p.xlarge is designed for consistent, non-burstable workloads such as lightweight application hosting, basic web serving, and development environments requiring dedicated CPU resources without high-throughput processing demands.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, delivering stable compute performance for standard workloads.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance offering balanced memory-to-core allocation and consistent CPU performance for non-burstable workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Dedicated CPU allocation ensuring consistent performance without burstable credits
Appropriate for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic application hosting
